reasoning: The question specifically states in its fine print that "A self-coup... will count. This includes scenarios in which an incumbent leader refuses to relinquish power after an election." This explicit inclusion criteria perfectly describes Nicolás Maduro's actions following the July 2024 Venezuelan presidential election. International observers and credible media reporting widely confirm that the opposition won the election by a wide margin, but Maduro refused to relinquish power and inaugurated himself in January 2025. The inclusion of this exact scenario in the resolution fine print strongly points to an intended 'YES' resolution for Venezuela's situation. Furthermore, even if Venezuela were somehow excluded (e.g., if resolvers rule his 2018 election means he didn't recently 'come to power legally'), there remains a significant base rate probability (~25%) of a successful standard military coup in Africa given the remaining ~2 months until July 1, 2025, and the high recent frequency of such events. This combination of a highly probable definitional match (Venezuela) and a robust background base rate yields a strong predictive confidence for a YES resolution.
